title: agentoffice/v1 frontmatter (AIP-22)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter portion of an AIP-22 OFFICE.md (workspace root or per-context view). The single doctype 'office.workspace/v1' is used in both modes; the host distinguishes by checking whether `extends` is set. Per-item-kind schemas are delegated to AIP-18 (COLLECTION.md / ITEM.md).

schema.description: Discriminator for the AIP-22 workspace doctype.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for the company or view.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of purpose: what this company is, who it serves.

version.description: Semantic version of the WORKSPACE shape. Bump on collection / orgTree / lint / defaults changes. Independent of the company's content version.

extends.description: OPTIONAL — relative path to a parent OFFICE.md. Presence makes the manifest a VIEW; absence makes it a WORKSPACE ROOT. Recursive composition; maximum chain depth is 8.

items.description: Either a ws:// ref to an AIP-9 operator or AIP-3 skill, or a relative path to a consumer workspace folder.

appliesTo.description: OPTIONAL — list of consumers this VIEW adapts the company for. Hosts MUST refuse the view if any binding does not resolve. Not inherited; views declare their own scope.

legalName.description: OPTIONAL — registered legal name of the entity, if it differs from the title.

legalEntity.description: OPTIONAL — ws:// ref to the legal entity OFFICE.md (self-ref if this manifest IS the legal entity; parent ref if this is a subsidiary view).

jurisdiction.description: OPTIONAL — ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for primary jurisdiction (e.g. US, FR, DE, GB).

foundedAt.description: OPTIONAL — ISO 8601 date the entity was founded.

defaultCurrency.description: OPTIONAL — ISO 4217 default currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, GBP).

identity.description: Organisation identity fields. Each leaf field independently overridable via merge; a divisional view MAY narrow `jurisdiction` while inheriting `mission` from the parent.

executor.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-9 default org-level operator. The host activates this operator for company-level prompts when no more-specific operator applies.

governance.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-7 policy or audit binding. May be a path to an AIP-7 policy file or a ws:// ref. Org-level approvals (role creation, reporting reassignment, jurisdiction change) flow through this ref. Subject to the office_signing_downgrade one-way switch when the bound policy declares signing.required=true.

work.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-20 work tracker the company runs. Cross-references on items resolve against the bound work workspace by default.

agency.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-21 agency context. Set when the company also operates as a commercial agency selling services to external counterparties.

knowledge.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-10 KNOWLEDGE.md ref. The institutional wiki — runbooks, onboarding docs, decision logs.

playbook.description: OPTIONAL — AIP-12 active playbook governing culture, values, and operating rhythm.

collections.description: Collections enabled by this organisation. Three forms supported: inline (full COLLECTION.md frontmatter), file ref, or registry import. Merge-by-effective-name (alias if set, otherwise the collection's name) across the extends chain.

enabled.description: Whether org-tree containment is active. ONE-WAY SWITCH: once true at any ancestor, descendants MUST NOT set false (would orphan items). Refusal: office_orgtree_disable (HARD).

field.description: Item field name carrying the org-parent ref. Defaults to AIP-18's universal-ish 'parent'.

allowedKinds.description: OPTIONAL — collection names that participate in the org tree. Items in collections NOT listed here are outside the tree (e.g. policies, objectives) and must not carry containment refs.

allowedParentKinds.description: OPTIONAL — containment matrix. Keys are CHILD collection names; values are arrays of allowed PARENT collection names. The host enforces this at item-write time. Per-item violations: office_orgtree_invalid_parent_kind (HARD).

maxDepth.description: OPTIONAL — maximum org-tree depth. ONE-WAY SWITCH on widening: once set at any ancestor, descendants may narrow (smaller value) but MUST NOT widen. Refusal: office_orgtree_depth_widen (HARD).

containment.description: Org-tree containment axis. AIP-22's distinctive concept: which collection kinds nest under which, how deep the tree goes.

enabled.description: Whether the reporting graph is active. When false, the host does not validate reportsTo refs.

field.description: Item field name carrying the reporting (manager) ref. Distinct from the containment field — a role's manager is logically separate from its containment parent.

cardinality.description: Reporting cardinality. 'single' (typical) = one manager per role; 'multiple' = matrixed (role MAY report to multiple managers, the field becomes an array).

mustResolveTo.description: Collection name the report target MUST resolve to. Typically 'role' (a role reports to another role). The host MUST refuse a reportsTo ref pointing at a different kind.

circularBan.description: Whether reporting cycles are banned. Default true. The host MUST walk the reportsTo chain on every write and refuse a write that would close a cycle (office_orgtree_circular_report, HARD).

reporting.description: Reporting / authority graph. Logically separate from containment; a role's manager is independent from its containment parent.

orgTree.description: Org-tree declaration — the AIP-22 distinctive concept. Containment governs the structural tree; reporting governs the authority graph.

id.description: Stable kebab-case lint id. Merge key when composing with extends parent.

kind.description: Workspace-spanning lint algorithm. AIP-18 per-collection lints (missing-owner, overdue, required-field, etc.) live on COLLECTION.md and are NOT redeclared here. 'custom' delegates to a host-defined check identified by `id`.

severity.description: Lint severity. Children may soften; governance policies MAY forbid softening below `error`.

params.description: Kind-specific parameters. e.g. { collections: [role] } for orphan-role; { days: 30 } for stale-objective.

workflow.description: OPTIONAL — default AIP-15 WORKFLOW.md path or ref. Routine workflow run against company items (e.g. monthly reporting-graph integrity sweep).

approvalClass.description: Approval class for mutations. 'auto' = no gate; 'always' = every mutation requires approval; 'on-mutate' = approval on field-level mutations; 'policy:<ref>' = delegate to an AIP-7 policy.

auditMutations.description: Whether mutations are audited. ONE-WAY SWITCH: once true at any ancestor, descendants MUST NOT set false. Refusal: office_audit_downgrade (HARD).

defaults.description: Routine workflow defaults. Composes with AIP-15.

homePage.description: OPTIONAL — id of the item to use as the company landing page (e.g. a top-level department or the founder role).

defaultView.description: Default rendering mode. 'tree' is the typical pick — org charts render naturally as trees.

display.description: Display hints for UIs that render the company. Runtime-agnostic.

metadata.description: Vendor-specific extensions, namespaced under <vendor>. Deep-merged across the extends chain. MUST NOT change the meaning of any spec field.

allOf.description: A view (any manifest with appliesTo set) MUST extend a parent. A workspace-root manifest has neither field.

collectionEntry.description: One collection declaration. Either an inline AIP-18 collection schema, or a ref (path or ws:// URI) optionally aliased and version-pinned.

inline.description: Full AIP-18 collection.schema/v1 frontmatter, parsed in-place. The host registers the collection directly via AIP-18's defineCollection without loading a separate file.

collectionInline.description: Inline collection declaration. Full AIP-18 schema embedded; hosts MUST validate it against the AIP-18 COLLECTION schema before registration.

ref.description: Either a relative path to a COLLECTION.md (file ref) or a ws://collections/<slug> URI (registry import). The host loads the referenced collection via AIP-18 and registers it under its name (or the alias, if set).

alias.description: OPTIONAL — workspace-local name to expose the collection under. Items in this organisation reference the alias, not the upstream name. Two collections resolving to the same effective name (alias or upstream) is a HARD failure: office_collection_alias_conflict.

version.description: OPTIONAL — semver range (e.g. "1.x", "^1.2", "1.2.0"). When set, schema bumps outside the range fail with collection_item_schema_pinned_drift (HARD) at item load time.

collectionRef.description: Collection ref declaration. Either a file path (./.. /COLLECTION.md) or a registry URI (ws://collections/<slug>).
